title: S3 Objects, Keys, Metadata, Tags, Listing, and Consistency · Go Semantics Case: Bucket Key Version Identity
summary: A team encodes tenant authority in free-form key strings, overwrites metadata accidentally, assumes folders are directories, and scans one unpaginated listing as a transaction. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bucket key version identity increment in S3 object-model and listing invariant table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Distinguish bucket, key, version ID, delete marker, upload ID, part number, and application identity.
Provide accessible file names, types, sizes, progress, cancellation, error recovery, transcripts or alternatives, and keyboard-operable controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Bucket Key Version Identity · Theory Bucket Key Version Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9318 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
